### Overview
You want inspirational and career-oriented talks, so I’m building a full Wednesday schedule around memorable, energizing sessions with strong speakers and big-picture stories, not dry executive keyword matches.

### Schedule
Wednesday, April 22, 2026
- Primary: From algorithms to agents: How Gen Z, Gen Alpha, and AI are rewiring commerce (id 3879152), 11:00-11:45, Lagoon B, Not full. Best opening pick for this vibe. Stephanie Wong gives this slot stronger narrative energy and broader appeal than the more corporate alternatives.
- Primary: Avery Dennison scales AI for product development and research excellence (id 3993506), 12:15-13:00, Not full. Good momentum session, real-world story, and more energizing than a dry platform update.
- Primary: The agentic ROI framework for lean teams (id 3912344), 13:30-14:15, Not full. Useful career framing without disappearing into implementation weeds.
- Primary: From blank page to final product using Google Workspace with Gemini (id 3920166), 14:30-15:15, Not full. Broad, approachable, and likely to keep energy high.
- Primary: Fireside chat: Agents and the post-app startup economy (id 3920157), 15:45-16:30, Not full. Strong future-facing slot with real conference energy.
- Primary: AI-driven transformation boosts growth for Macy’s and Openreach (id 4070693), 17:00-17:45, Not full. Best closer, memorable and broadly relevant.

### Tradeoffs
- I deprioritized drier executive-corporate sessions even when they matched more business keywords.
- Stephanie Wong stays in because this request is about inspiration and career energy, not just leader-language density.
